text: Oxidative stress is one of the core pathogenic factors of SAP[3,4], significantly contributing to pancreatic cell damage, amplification of inflammatory responses, and subsequent multiple organ dysfunction[5,6]. Peroxiredoxin 1 (Prdx1), as a key member of the peroxiredoxin family[7], is traditionally considered an intracellular antioxidant enzyme that scavenges reactive oxygen species (ROS) and alleviates oxidative damage[8,9]. Our previous studies have confirmed that intracellular Prdx1 plays a crucial role in maintaining the antioxidant capacity of pancreatic acinar cells in AP[10]. Quercetin is a natural flavonoid compound and is widely known for its potent free radical scavenging and antioxidant properties[11]. Furthermore, recent evidence suggests that quercetin can induce Prdx1 expression[12] and has shown efficacy in alleviating AP symptoms[13,14], making it a promising therapeutic candidate for SAP. However, quercetin's inherent poor solubility and low bioavailability severely limit its therapeutic application[15], highlighting the urgent need for sophisticated drug delivery systems.
